name: NC Food and Nutrition Services (SNAP)
description: North Carolina's SNAP program provides monthly benefits on an EBT card to help low-income households purchase food. Formerly known as Food Stamps.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income individuals and families meeting federal income guidelines
incomeLimitNotes: Gross income at or below 200% FPL for most households; net income at or below 100% FPL
hours: County DSS offices: Mon-Fri 8:00 AM - 5:00 PM typically

name: NC ePASS (Electronic Pre-Assessment Screening Service)
description: Online portal for applying for Food and Nutrition Services (SNAP), Medicaid, Work First, child care subsidy, and energy assistance programs in North Carolina.
eligibilitySummary: Any North Carolina resident can use ePASS to screen for and apply for multiple benefit programs
incomeLimitNotes: Varies by program applied for
hours: Online portal available 24/7

name: NC Medicaid
description: North Carolina's Medicaid program provides free or low-cost health coverage to eligible low-income adults, children, pregnant women, elderly, and people with disabilities.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income adults, children, pregnant women, elderly, and individuals with disabilities
incomeLimitNotes: Adults 19-64: up to 138% FPL; Children: up to 216% FPL; Pregnant women: up to 196% FPL
hours: NC Medicaid Contact Center: Mon-Fri 8:00 AM - 5:00 PM

name: NC Medicaid Managed Care Plans
description: Portal for NC Medicaid beneficiaries to learn about and choose from available managed care health plans, access benefits information, and find providers.
eligibilitySummary: NC Medicaid beneficiaries enrolled in managed care
incomeLimitNotes: Must be enrolled in NC Medicaid
hours: Mon-Fri 7:00 AM - 7:00 PM

name: NC Division of Employment Security (Unemployment Insurance)
description: Administers North Carolina's unemployment insurance program providing temporary financial assistance to workers who lost jobs through no fault of their own.
eligibilitySummary: Workers who lost employment through no fault of their own, earned sufficient wages, and are actively seeking work
incomeLimitNotes: Must have earned at least $780 in the highest quarter of the base period
hours: Phone: Mon-Fri 8:00 AM - 4:30 PM; Online claims available 24/7

name: NCWorks Online
description: North Carolina's statewide workforce system with 70+ career centers offering job search tools, resume building, career counseling, training programs, and employment services.
eligibilitySummary: All North Carolina job seekers and employers; specialized services for veterans, youth, and individuals with barriers to employment
hours: Online portal 24/7; Career Centers typically Mon-Fri 8:00 AM - 5:00 PM

name: NC Commerce Workforce Development
description: Oversees North Carolina's workforce development programs including job training, apprenticeships, and employment services through the Division of Workforce Solutions.
eligibilitySummary: North Carolina job seekers, workers, and employers
hours: Mon-Fri 8:00 AM - 5:00 PM

name: North Carolina Housing Finance Agency (NCHFA)
description: Creates affordable housing opportunities for North Carolinians including rental assistance, homebuyer programs, and housing for people with disabilities or experiencing homelessness.
eligibilitySummary: Low- and moderate-income North Carolinians; individuals with disabilities; persons experiencing homelessness
incomeLimitNotes: Varies by program; generally targets households at or below 80% AMI
hours: Mon-Fri 8:00 AM - 5:00 PM

name: NCHFA Rental Assistance Programs
description: Provides rental assistance including Key Rental Assistance, Targeting Program, and Transitions to Community Living Vouchers for people with disabilities or experiencing homelessness.
eligibilitySummary: People with disabilities or experiencing homelessness with very low or extremely low incomes
incomeLimitNotes: Generally at or below 30% AMI for Key Rental Assistance
hours: Mon-Fri 8:00 AM - 5:00 PM

name: Legal Aid of North Carolina
description: Nonprofit law firm providing free civil legal assistance to low-income individuals and communities across all 100 North Carolina counties in areas including housing, family law, public benefits, and consumer issues.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income individuals at or below 125-200% of the federal poverty level, depending on program
incomeLimitNotes: Generally at or below 125% FPL; some programs up to 200% FPL
hours: Mon-Fri 8:30 AM - 5:00 PM; Helpline hours may vary

name: Pisgah Legal Services
description: Nonprofit law firm providing free civil legal aid to low-income people in Western North Carolina, helping nearly 28,000 people annually with housing, safety from abuse, healthcare, and income issues.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income residents of Western North Carolina counties including Buncombe, Madison, Transylvania, Henderson, Polk, and Rutherford
incomeLimitNotes: Generally at or below 125% FPL; some programs have higher limits
hours: Mon-Fri 8:30 AM - 5:00 PM

name: LawHelpNC.org
description: Online guide to free and low-cost legal aid, assistance, and services in North Carolina. Helps users find legal help organizations and self-help legal information by topic and location.
eligibilitySummary: Any North Carolina resident seeking legal information or referral to free legal services
hours: Online resource available 24/7

name: NC Low Income Energy Assistance Program (LIEAP)
description: Federally-funded program providing a one-time payment to help eligible low-income households pay heating bills during winter months.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income households with income at or below 130% of the federal poverty level
incomeLimitNotes: At or below 130% FPL; priority given to households with elderly (60+) or disabled members. Benefit: $300-$500
hours: Applications accepted Dec 1 - March 31 (seniors/disabled from Dec 1; all others from Jan 1)

name: NC Crisis Intervention Program (CIP)
description: Federally-funded program providing emergency assistance to low-income households experiencing a heating or cooling crisis, such as disconnection of utility service or fuel shortage.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income households experiencing a heating or cooling emergency
incomeLimitNotes: At or below 150% FPL
hours: Contact local county DSS; generally Mon-Fri 8:00 AM - 5:00 PM

name: NC 211
description: Free, confidential, multilingual health and human services information and referral service available 24/7 in all 100 North Carolina counties, managed by United Way of North Carolina.
eligibilitySummary: Any North Carolina resident in need of information about community resources and services
hours: 24 hours a day, 7 days a week

name: United Way of North Carolina
description: Statewide nonprofit organization that manages NC 211 and supports local United Ways across all 100 counties, connecting residents to essential health and human services.
address: 6404 Falls of Neuse Rd Suite 100, Raleigh, NC 27615
eligibilitySummary: All North Carolina residents seeking community resources and support
hours: Mon-Fri 8:30 AM - 5:00 PM

name: Food Bank of Central & Eastern North Carolina
description: Feeding America affiliate serving 34 counties in central and eastern NC through 700+ pantries, no-cost markets, meal sites, and delivery programs. Ranks in the top 15 nationally among food banks.
eligibilitySummary: Individuals and families facing hunger in 34 counties of central and eastern North Carolina
hours: Mon-Fri 8:00 AM - 5:00 PM; distribution hours vary by location

name: Second Harvest Food Bank of Northwest North Carolina
description: Feeding America affiliate serving 18 counties in Northwest NC through nearly 500 local food assistance programs, providing food, nutrition education, and culinary career training.
eligibilitySummary: Individuals and families facing food insecurity in 18 counties of Northwest North Carolina
hours: Mon-Fri 8:00 AM - 5:00 PM; distribution hours vary by partner location

name: MANNA FoodBank
description: Feeding America affiliate working to end food insecurity in 16 counties of Western North Carolina and the Qualla Boundary through partner agencies and direct food distribution.
eligibilitySummary: Individuals and families facing hunger in 16 counties of Western North Carolina including the Qualla Boundary
hours: Mon-Fri 8:00 AM - 4:30 PM; distribution hours vary

name: NC Work First Family Assistance (TANF)
description: North Carolina's Temporary Assistance for Needy Families (TANF) program providing short-term cash assistance, job training, and supportive services to help parents become employed and self-sufficient.
eligibilitySummary: Families with dependent children whose income and resources fall below program limits
incomeLimitNotes: Income must be below 50% FPL; benefits limited to 24 months after entering work components
hours: County DSS offices: Mon-Fri 8:00 AM - 5:00 PM

name: NC WIC Program (Women, Infants, and Children)
description: Provides supplemental nutrition, breastfeeding support, nutrition education, and referrals for health care and community services to eligible pregnant, postpartum, and breastfeeding women, infants, and children up to age 5.
eligibilitySummary: Pregnant, postpartum, and breastfeeding women, infants, and children up to age 5 who are at nutritional risk
incomeLimitNotes: At or below 185% FPL; automatically eligible if receiving Medicaid, SNAP, or Work First
hours: Contact local WIC clinic; typically Mon-Fri 8:00 AM - 5:00 PM

name: NC Child Care Subsidy Program
description: Uses state and federal funds to provide subsidized child care services to eligible families, helping parents afford quality child care while they work or attend school.
eligibilitySummary: Families with children age 0-12 (or up to 18 for special needs) who are working, in school, or in training and meet income requirements
incomeLimitNotes: At or below 200% FPL; families pay a co-payment based on income
hours: County DSS offices: Mon-Fri 8:00 AM - 5:00 PM

name: NC DCDEE Child Care Financial Assistance
description: Division of Child Development and Early Education portal with information on child care financial assistance eligibility, application process, and how the subsidy program works.
eligibilitySummary: Families meeting income and activity requirements seeking help paying for licensed child care
incomeLimitNotes: At or below 200% FPL
hours: Online resource available 24/7

name: NC Department of Military and Veterans Affairs (DMVA)
description: Supports military service members, veterans, and their families through benefits claims assistance, employment services, education support, and veteran service offices across the state.
eligibilitySummary: Military veterans, active service members, reservists, National Guard members, and their families
hours: Mon-Fri 8:00 AM - 5:00 PM

name: NC DMVA Veterans Affairs Division - Benefits & Claims
description: Provides qualified benefits specialists in 12 offices statewide to help veterans file claims and access VA benefits, disability compensation, pensions, and other entitlements.
eligibilitySummary: Veterans and their surviving dependents seeking VA benefits and claims assistance
hours: Mon-Fri 8:00 AM - 5:00 PM

name: NCDOT Public Transit Services
description: Oversees 98 public transportation systems statewide, administering state and federal funds for public transit and providing information on available transit options across North Carolina.
eligibilitySummary: All North Carolina residents; various local transit systems may have specific eligibility for reduced fares
hours: Varies by local transit system

name: GoTriangle (Triangle Region Transit)
description: Regional public transit system serving the Research Triangle area including Raleigh, Durham, Chapel Hill, Cary, and Apex with bus, express, and paratransit services.
eligibilitySummary: All riders in the Triangle region; reduced fares for seniors, persons with disabilities, and Medicare cardholders
hours: Service hours vary by route; customer service Mon-Fri 7:00 AM - 7:00 PM

name: Charlotte Area Transit System (CATS)
description: Public transit system offering bus, light rail (LYNX Blue Line), streetcar (CityLYNX Gold Line), vanpool, and paratransit services in the Charlotte-Mecklenburg area.
eligibilitySummary: All riders; reduced fares for seniors 62+, persons with disabilities, and Medicare cardholders
hours: Customer service: Mon-Fri 6:00 AM - 8:00 PM, Sat 8:00 AM - 6:00 PM

name: NC Division of Employment and Independence for People with Disabilities (EIPD/DVRS)
description: Helps people with disabilities achieve employment and independence through vocational counseling, education, training, job placement, assistive technology, and individualized employment plans.
eligibilitySummary: Individuals with physical or mental disabilities that create barriers to employment
incomeLimitNotes: No income requirements to apply; services are free
hours: Mon-Fri 8:00 AM - 5:00 PM

name: NC DHHS Crisis Services (988 Suicide and Crisis Lifeline)
description: Free, private behavioral health crisis services available 24/7 through calling or texting 988 for suicide prevention, mental health crises, and substance use emergencies.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone experiencing a mental health crisis, suicidal thoughts, or substance use emergency
hours: 24 hours a day, 7 days a week

name: NC Coalition Against Domestic Violence (NCCADV)
description: Statewide organization supporting domestic violence agencies in all 100 NC counties. Provides referrals to local crisis hotlines, emergency shelter, counseling, and support services for survivors.
eligibilitySummary: Survivors of domestic violence and their families; does not provide direct services but connects to local agencies
hours: National DV Hotline: 24/7; local agency hours vary

name: Seniors' Health Insurance Information Program (SHIIP)
description: Free, unbiased counseling for Medicare beneficiaries about Medicare, Medicare supplements, Medicare Advantage, Medicare Part D, and long-term care insurance through counselors in every NC county.
eligibilitySummary: Medicare beneficiaries, seniors, and their caregivers
hours: Mon-Fri 8:00 AM - 5:00 PM; local counseling by appointment

name: NC Community Action Association
description: Network of 34 Community Action Agencies across North Carolina providing anti-poverty services including emergency assistance, housing, education, and employment support.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income individuals and families; eligibility varies by local Community Action Agency and program
incomeLimitNotes: Varies by program; generally at or below 125-200% FPL
hours: Mon-Fri 8:30 AM - 5:00 PM; local agency hours vary

name: NC Longleaf Commitment Grant
description: Grant program providing community college tuition assistance for eligible North Carolina students, covering $700 to $2,800 per year for up to two years at any of the state's 58 community colleges.
eligibilitySummary: Recent NC high school graduates attending a NC community college who file the FAFSA and demonstrate financial need
incomeLimitNotes: Must file FAFSA and demonstrate unmet financial need; not a separate application
hours: Contact individual community college financial aid offices

name: Mecklenburg County Department of Community Resources (DCR)
description: Provides access to food assistance, energy assistance, employment support, housing assistance, Medicaid, and child support services through Community Resource Centers in Mecklenburg County.
eligibilitySummary: Mecklenburg County residents in need of social services and community resources
incomeLimitNotes: Varies by program
hours: Mon-Fri 8:00 AM - 5:00 PM

name: Mecklenburg County DSS
description: Provides financial assistance, child welfare services, adult protective services, and other social services to individuals and families in Mecklenburg County.
eligibilitySummary: Mecklenburg County residents in need of social services
incomeLimitNotes: Varies by program
hours: Mon-Fri 8:00 AM - 5:00 PM

name: CharMeck 311
description: Customer contact center for the City of Charlotte and Mecklenburg County, providing information about city/county services, accepting service requests, and connecting residents to government resources.
eligibilitySummary: All Charlotte and Mecklenburg County residents
hours: Mon-Fri 7:00 AM - 7:00 PM

name: Mecklenburg County Energy Assistance
description: Helps eligible low-income households in Mecklenburg County with energy costs including LIEAP heating assistance and Crisis Intervention Program for utility emergencies.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income Mecklenburg County households facing heating or cooling emergencies or needing energy bill assistance
incomeLimitNotes: LIEAP: at or below 130% FPL; CIP: at or below 150% FPL
hours: Mon-Fri 8:00 AM - 5:00 PM

name: Mecklenburg County Work First Family Assistance
description: Mecklenburg County's TANF program providing temporary cash assistance, employment services, and support to help families with children achieve self-sufficiency.
eligibilitySummary: Families with dependent children in Mecklenburg County who meet income and resource limits
incomeLimitNotes: Income must be below 50% FPL
hours: Mon-Fri 8:00 AM - 5:00 PM

name: Mecklenburg County WIC Program
description: Provides supplemental food, nutrition education, breastfeeding support, and health referrals to eligible pregnant women, new mothers, infants, and children under 5 in Mecklenburg County.
eligibilitySummary: Pregnant, postpartum, and breastfeeding women, infants, and children under 5 in Mecklenburg County at nutritional risk
incomeLimitNotes: At or below 185% FPL; automatically eligible if on Medicaid, SNAP, or Work First
hours: Mon-Fri; hours vary by clinic location

name: NC DHHS Energy Assistance Programs
description: Central information hub for all North Carolina energy assistance programs including LIEAP, Crisis Intervention Program, and Weatherization Assistance Program.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income North Carolina households; eligibility varies by specific program
incomeLimitNotes: LIEAP: 130% FPL; CIP: 150% FPL; WAP: 200% FPL
hours: Contact local county DSS

name: Food Bank of Central & Eastern NC - Find Food
description: Online tool to locate nearby food pantries, no-cost markets, meal sites, and food distribution programs across 34 counties in central and eastern North Carolina.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone experiencing food insecurity in central and eastern NC; no documentation required at most sites
hours: Online tool 24/7; distribution hours vary by location

name: NC Medicaid Application Portal
description: Online portal for applying for NC Medicaid, checking application status, finding a Medicaid Ambassador for help, or calling for application assistance.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income North Carolinians including adults, children, pregnant women, elderly, and disabled individuals
incomeLimitNotes: Varies by category; adults up to 138% FPL under Medicaid expansion
hours: NC Navigator appointments available; online applications 24/7
